column8: Mutant fails on import. LLM misunderstands the experiment format and overcomplicates the experiments. It detects the mutant anyways, then doesn't translate the experiment into a test correctly and confuses itself.

Mutant fails on import. LLM first fails to import correctly, then fixes the imports but also recreates the mutant. After a while it removes the recreated mutant, then has to fix an assertion, but arrives at a correct test.

Mutant fails on import. LLM first runs into an error when importing the mutant, doesn't realize that this kills the mutant, then recreates a (wrong) version of the mutant that doesn't fail to compile and tests its version of the mutant instead of the real mutant.
Mutant fails on import. LLM first fails to import correctly, then instantly finds a killing test.

Mutant fails on import. LLM interprets this as the experiment failing, then does a couple of experiments without importing the mutant. Then adds the mutant again and iterprets the output as the experiment failing again. Then writes "As a result, we cannot test the mutant implementation directly due to the failure to import it successfully." and tries to write a test, which fails because of a wrong assertion.
Mutant fails on import. LLM tries a test, has to fix an assertion, then succeeds.
Mutant fails on import. LLM correctly identifies the mutant, but then recreates the mutant and fails to create a killing test, since it's testing the recreated mutant.
Mutant fails on import. LLM fails a couple test attempts due to a wrong assertion, then changes the assertion and succeeds.
LLM tries two inputs that don't detect the mutant, then calls it equivalent.
LLM fails to write a killing test for a while, keeps adjusting the test slightly, then gets a killing test on the 12th try.

LLM tries experiments with various inputs without even importing the mutant, instead trying to a regex to match, so the mutated function is called. It then tries one test with the mutant, then declares it equivalent.
LLM bypasses the regex by importing a private class directly. Then kill the mutant. Even uses mocking to do so!
The mutant changes the allowed email address length in a "is_email()" function. The LLM fails to create a valid email address that fails the length check.
LLM doesn't tries a couple of tests until it finds the right email address to detect the mutant.
Killing the mutant requires passing an optional parameter to the target function. The LLM tries good inputs, but never passes the optional parameter.
LLM uses wrong imports for the first test, then finds a working test right away by passing the optional parameter.
The mutant changes the "is_isbn_13()" function, but the LLM only tests the "is_isbn_10()" function. The LLM also omits all imports in exactly every 2nd experiment, leading to a lot of failures.
LLM uses wrong imports for the first test, then finds a working test right away.
LLM tries a couple of inputs, then declares the mutant as equivalent.
LLM uses wrong imports for the first test, then finds a working test right away.
Killing the mutant requires omitting an optional parameter when calling the target function. The LLM tries good inputs, but never omits the optional parameter.
LLM uses wrong imports for the first test, then finds a working test right away.
LLM detects the mutant in the first experiment, but confuses itself by using very strange verifying expressions, then claims the mutant is equivalent.
LLM uses wrong imports for the first test, then finds a working test right away.
